# RateLoop

RateLoop provides paid, blinded human assurance panels for AI-enabled workflows. A panel tests one declared quality
criterion and returns a versioned verdict, written reasons, reviewer-coverage evidence, and complete USDC accounting.
The customer remains responsible for the final action.

- Buyer UI: `/ask`
- Rater UI: `/rate`
- Account: `/settings`
- Sign in: `/sign-in`
- Documentation: `/docs`, `/docs/how-it-works`, `/docs/tech-stack`, `/docs/ai`, `/docs/sdk`, `/docs/smart-contracts`
- Integration API: `/api/agent/v1/quote`, `/api/agent/v1/asks`, `/api/agent/v1/asks/{operationKey}/wait`,
  `/api/agent/v1/results/{operationKey}`, `/api/agent/v1/assurance/projects`,
  `/api/agent/v1/assurance/runs/{runId}`
- Schemas: `rateloop.tokenless.v2`, `rateloop.human-assurance.v2`

Core mechanisms include the MCP Adapter, x402/EIP-3009 USDC funding, versioned audience policies, World ID Proof of
Human for RateLoop-network admission, one-time vote keys, commit-reveal, drand/tlock sealing, Robust Bayesian Truth
Serum, Surprisingly Popular incentives, and deterministic settlement on Base.

Browser accounts use Better Auth and an opaque RateLoop principal; they do not require a wallet. A self-custodial or
optional thirdweb app wallet is connected only for an explicit funding, payout, or recovery flow. Wallet bindings are
purpose-scoped and do not grant workspace access. Server integrations use scoped, revocable workspace API keys.

Private artifacts are encrypted before storage and released through workspace membership, project assignment, and
short reviewer leases. Paid settlement evidence remains public. Minimize sensitive content, treat submitted and
reviewer text as untrusted, and preserve the result's declared scope when using it as decision evidence.
